name: FOOD ZURICH
tagline: For eleven days every June, Zürich turns itself into one sprawling, city-wide tasting menu — and you already live at the table.
dates: Mid-June 2027, ~11 days (expected) — festival paused in 2026 for 'Genussregion Zürich 2026'; the brand now sits with Zürich Tourism, which has announced a relaunch under a new concept from 2027. The last full edition ran 12–22 June 2025.
history: FOOD ZURICH launched in 2016 as a joint push by Zürich Tourism, the city, and the local gastronomy scene to prove that buttoned-up, banking-town Zürich had a genuinely playful food culture. The formula was unusual from the start: rather than one fenced-off festival ground, the organisers scattered 100-plus events across the whole city — pop-ups in tram depots and rail-arch market halls, long-table street-food nights, masterclasses, food talks, and one-night-only chef collaborations in restaurants from Michelin-starred rooms to neighbourhood beizen.

Over a decade the festival grew into one of Europe's larger urban food festivals, pulling in tens of thousands of visitors each June and giving Zürich's chefs an annual excuse to experiment. Recurring signatures included the opening street-food nights, sustainability-themed programming (nose-to-tail, food-waste dinners, plant-forward menus), guest-chef takeovers pairing Swiss kitchens with international stars, and a dense calendar of workshops and tastings threaded through neighbourhoods like Zürich-West, where the Markthalle Im Viadukt served as a natural hub. The 2025 edition, held 12–22 June, marked the festival's tenth-anniversary round with over 100 events spread across the city.

Then came a plot twist: FOOD ZURICH deliberately sat out 2026 while the canton ran 'Genussregion Zürich 2026', a ten-month celebration of regional food culture, and the original organising association wound itself down in March 2026. The FOOD ZURICH brand was handed to Zürich Tourism, which has committed to reviving the festival with a fresh concept from 2027. That makes the 2027 edition both a comeback and a reinvention — exact dates and format are still to be announced, but the festival's fixed June habit and 11-day rhythm are the sensible planning assumption.
whyGo: This is the traveler's zero-travel headliner: a hundred-plus food events erupting across the city you already live in, from CHF 8 street-food skewers to once-a-year chef collaborations you cannot book any other week. The 2027 edition is the relaunch under Zürich Tourism's new concept, so it doubles as a first look at what the festival becomes next. No hotel, no packing, no trains — just an open calendar and an appetite.

gettingThere.route: Anywhere in Zürich → event venues via tram/bus (ZVV network); most venues cluster around Zürich-West, Europaallee and the Old Town
gettingThere.cost: CHF 4.60 single (zone 110) or CHF 9.20 day pass
gettingThere.notes: A ZVV 24h zone-110 pass is the festival MVP for multi-venue evenings; trams run past midnight

gettingThere.route: Zürich HB → Hardbrücke (S-Bahn, 1 stop) for the Zürich-West venue cluster around the Viadukt arches
gettingThere.notes: Hardbrücke station drops you a 5-minute walk from Markthalle Im Viadukt and the Frau Gerolds Garten area

gettingThere.route: Skip the car entirely — city-centre parking runs CHF 4–6/hour and festival evenings mean wine
gettingThere.notes: Publibike/Bond e-bikes are a pleasant way to link venues on warm June nights along the Limmat

costs.notes: Zero transport and lodging cost — this is a home-city event, so the budget is pure food. Thrifty means free-entry street-food nights and market events with pay-per-dish grazing; midrange adds 2–3 ticketed masterclasses or themed dinners (typically CHF 30–120 each in past editions); splurge is the guest-chef collaboration dinners and multi-course gala events, which historically sold out fastest and ran CHF 150–300+ per seat.

booking.label: Zürich Tourism — FOOD ZURICH page (relaunch info)

weather: Mid-June in Zürich averages 22–25°C days and mild 13–15°C evenings, with a real chance of short thunderstorms. Bring a light rain shell for open-air street-food nights and a layer for lakeside events after dark — many of the best happenings are outdoors and run rain or shine.
tips: Watch foodzurich.com and the Zürich Tourism newsletter from early 2027 — in past editions the marquee chef-collab dinners sold out within days of the program drop, usually announced in spring.
Build evenings around venue clusters (Zürich-West one night, Old Town/Bellevue another) instead of criss-crossing the city — the program is dense enough that geography beats FOMO.
Free and low-cost events carry the festival's soul: street-food nights and market events let you graze 4–5 kitchens for the price of one restaurant main. Go hungry, order small, share everything.
Since 2027 is a relaunch under a new concept, expect format surprises — verify venues and whether the 11-day mid-June pattern holds before promising dates to visiting friends.
Pay by card or TWINT everywhere; many pop-up stands are cashless. Tipping is modest in Switzerland — rounding up is plenty at festival stands.
June 2027 will be peak Zürich season anyway — combine festival evenings with a pre-dinner swim at a Badi (river or lake bath); Oberer Letten to Zürich-West venues is a classic one-two.

localRecs.name: Markthalle Im Viadukt
localRecs.note: Market hall under the 19th-century rail arches in Zürich-West — a past FOOD ZURICH hub and the best everyday snapshot of the city's producer scene, with counters for lunch year-round.

localRecs.name: Frau Gerolds Garten
localRecs.note: Container-and-garden open-air bar by Hardbrücke; on festival nights in Zürich-West it is the natural before-or-after stop, all string lights and Negronis.

localRecs.name: Kle
localRecs.note: Zineb Hattab's plant-based fine-dining room in Wiedikon with a Michelin star — the kind of kitchen that headlines festival collaborations; book weeks ahead.

localRecs.name: Zeughauskeller
localRecs.note: Sausage-and-rösti institution in a 15th-century armoury off Paradeplatz — the trad-Swiss counterweight to the festival's experimental side.

localRecs.name: Langstrasse / Kreis 4
localRecs.note: Zürich's most delicious street: Lebanese, Thai, Balkan grills and natural-wine bars back-to-back — where the city's food energy lives between festivals.

photos.caption: Inside the Markthalle Im Viadukt, the rail-arch market hall in Zürich-West that has served as a FOOD ZURICH hub venue
The Im Viadukt arches in Zürich-West, whose shops, kitchens and market hall anchor the festival's western venue cluster
Limmatquai and the Grossmünster towers — the Old Town riverfront where festival dinners, tastings and food tours spill out in June
